_metadata.description: Controlled vocabulary for the 14 user-profile health conditions referenced by interaction_rules.condition_rules and clinical_risk_taxonomy.conditions. The user-facing label was previously hardcoded in lib/core/constants/schema_ids.dart conditionLabels — migrating gives clinician control of the user-facing condition copy. Lean schema (no display contract): id + name + notes + (optional) icd10 references + synonyms.
_metadata.purpose: Single source of truth for user-profile condition labels and descriptions. Drives condition-triggered interaction warnings (e.g. pregnancy + retinol → contraindicated). The `name` is shown in onboarding and the profile screen; `notes` shown in tap-to-learn.
_metadata.alignment: Pipeline source: scripts/data/clinical_risk_taxonomy.json `conditions` list, scripts/data/ingredient_interaction_rules.json `condition_rules[].condition_id`. Flutter consumer: lib/core/constants/schema_ids.dart `conditions` + `conditionLabels` (replaces hardcoded map).

field_contract.id: Stable snake_case identifier shared with the user profile and interaction rules.
field_contract.name: User-facing label shown in onboarding and the profile screen.
field_contract.notes: ≤200-char plain-English description shown in tap-to-learn modal.
field_contract.synonyms: Optional. Common alternative names users may recognize.
field_contract.icd10: Optional. ICD-10 code references for clinical context (e.g. {'code': 'O00-O9A', 'description': 'Pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium'}).

conditions.name: Pregnancy
conditions.notes: Currently pregnant. Many supplements have specific dose limits or are contraindicated; some interact with maternal-fetal physiology.

conditions.name: Breastfeeding
conditions.notes: Currently nursing. Some supplements pass into breast milk and can affect the infant; others alter milk supply.

icd10.description: Encounter for care and examination of lactating mother

conditions.name: Trying to Conceive
conditions.notes: Actively attempting pregnancy. Pre-conception nutrition matters for fetal development; some supplements impact fertility.

conditions.name: Upcoming Surgery
conditions.notes: Surgery planned in the near term. Several supplements increase bleeding risk or interact with anesthesia and must be paused 1-2 weeks pre-op.

conditions.name: High Blood Pressure
conditions.notes: Diagnosed elevated blood pressure. Some supplements raise BP (licorice, ephedra) or interfere with antihypertensive medications.

conditions.name: Heart Disease
conditions.notes: History of cardiac disease (coronary artery disease, heart failure, arrhythmia). Several supplements affect rhythm, contractility, or interact with cardiac medications.

conditions.name: Diabetes
conditions.notes: Type 1, type 2, or pre-diabetes. Many supplements affect blood sugar; some potentiate hypoglycemic medications and risk a low.

conditions.name: Bleeding Disorders
conditions.notes: Hemophilia, von Willebrand disease, low platelets, or on chronic anticoagulation. Avoid supplements that further inhibit platelet function or clotting (vitamin E high-dose, fish oil, ginkgo).

icd10.description: Coagulation defects, purpura and other hemorrhagic conditions

conditions.name: Kidney Disease
conditions.notes: Chronic kidney disease (any stage). Several supplements accumulate when renal clearance is reduced; some directly damage the kidneys at high doses.

icd10.description: Acute kidney failure and chronic kidney disease

conditions.name: Liver Disease
conditions.notes: Hepatitis, cirrhosis, fatty liver, or elevated liver enzymes. Avoid hepatotoxic supplements (kava, comfrey, high-dose niacin); many supplements need dose adjustment.

conditions.name: Thyroid Condition
conditions.notes: Hypothyroid, hyperthyroid, or on thyroid hormone replacement. Iodine, kelp, soy isoflavones, and biotin can alter thyroid hormone levels or assay results.

conditions.name: Autoimmune Condition
conditions.notes: Lupus, rheumatoid arthritis, MS, IBD, or similar. Immune-stimulating supplements (echinacea, astragalus, high-dose vitamin C) may worsen disease activity.

conditions.name: Epilepsy/Seizures
conditions.notes: Diagnosed seizure disorder. Several supplements lower seizure threshold (high-dose evening primrose, GABA, ginkgo) or interact with antiepileptic drugs.

conditions.name: High Cholesterol
conditions.notes: Diagnosed dyslipidemia or on a statin. Red yeast rice contains natural lovastatin and shouldn't combine with prescribed statins; niacin and fish oil affect lipid panel.

icd10.description: Disorders of lipoprotein metabolism and other lipidemias
